body.title: CLOSED: CC+/CC- causal rework tally in slate + context-load injection
body.result: CC tally added to context-load Step 5.8; working_together.md updated with CC+/CC- mechanism; pushed to agent_datacenter
body.description:
No mechanism exists to distinguish 'rework because design changed' (CC+, not Claude's fault) from 'rework because Claude was incomplete' (CC-, Claude's gap). Without this signal, CC has no way to improve on the specific class of failures it caused. Naming it in-moment creates the accountability that retrospective review misses.

Proposed shape: add '## CC score' section to today's slate. Each event is one line: 'CC+ YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM: <reason>' or 'CC- YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM: <reason>'. /context-load reads the slate (it already does) and injects a summary line after the In-flight block: 'Current slate: CC+ N / CC- N' plus last 3 events. When no CC score section exists, inject nothing (clean slate). Update docs/working_together.md in ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter to document the mechanism.

**Affected files:** ~/.claude/skills/context-load (inject CC tally after In-flight), ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/docs/working_together.md (document mechanism)
**Design rules:** none
**Scope boundary:** slate text format extension + context-load injection. NOT changing savestate or any other skill — /fixit handles writing (T-cc-plus-minus-fixit-tag).
**Test plan:** manually append a CC- line to today's slate; run /context-load; verify 'Current slate: CC+ 0 / CC- 1' appears in the context-load output.
**Pass condition:** /context-load session briefing shows CC tally when slate has CC score entries; shows nothing when slate has no CC score section.
